gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.title: The Shortlist - Founder Showcase
gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.summary: Gary's Guide: The Shortlist NYC - June Founder Showcase (#6) The Shortlist is back for our monthly Founder Showcase & the room keeps getting better. This is a monthly curated, application-only evening connecting early-stage founders with exceptional operators across New York. No panels. No pitches to VCs. Just six founders building breakout companies, a room full of people who've built things themselves, & the kind of candid conversation you don't get at most events. Each founder will share what they're building, why it matters, & the specific roles they're actively hiring for right now - followed by a mixer. What to expect: Founders presenting at the seed through Series B stage. Operators, builders, & executives who've seen what good looks like. An intentionally small room designed for real connection. Who this is for: Exceptional operators - whether you're actively exploring your next role, casually curious, or simply want to stay close to what's being built in NYC right now. Founders will be announced in the days leading up to the event. Attendance is intentionally limited. Apply to be considered. More information on The Shortlist can be found here. Founded by Andrew Yeung & Ivor Straford.

gary_2026_06_23_building_b2b_fintech_inf.title: Building B2B Fintech Infrastructure Stack - Rooftop Mixer
gary_2026_06_23_building_b2b_fintech_inf.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_23_building_b2b_fintech_inf.summary: Gary's Guide: Rutter, Extend, & Orb are hosting a private happy hour at Monarch Rooftop. Join engineers, operators, & builders from leading fintechs companies across NYC! Drinks & light bites will be provided. Feel free to bring a colleague but have them RSVP separately (space is limited)! About Rutter: Unified API platform that helps banks & fintechs automate supplier enablement using ERP data. By ingesting & normalizing payables data across accounting, commerce, & payments systems, Rutter enables accurate cardability analysis & automated supplier activation to turn eligible spend into card volume. Trusted by leading B2B fintechs including Mercury, PayPal, Airwallex, Capital One, & more than 100 companies. About Extend: Extend is the document infrastructure for agents. Fintech & Vertical AI teams like Mercury, Brex, Square, & more use Extend's API to parse, split, extract, & edit complex documents at scale. About Orb: Orb is the revenue design platform for AI & SaaS companies. Built on raw usage data, Orb unifies pricing, billing, & revenue intelligence-turning complex usage into accurate invoices & audit-ready revenue data, so companies can evolve how they monetize as fast as they ship. Leading companies like Vercel, Replit, Glean, & WRITER trust Orb to power their revenue. #nyc-fintech #b2b-saas #payments #api #financial-infrastructure #fintech-builders #AI #fintech

gary_2026_06_24_nyc_ai_demos.title: NYC AI Demos
gary_2026_06_24_nyc_ai_demos.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_24_nyc_ai_demos.place: The Refinery at Domino, 300 Kent Ave, Brooklyn
gary_2026_06_24_nyc_ai_demos.summary: Gary's Guide: NYC AI Demos returns to The Refinery at Domino on June 24 for its tenth installment for one more can't-miss evening before we break for summer. This special edition features a hand-picked lineup of fast-growing startups selected by some of New York's most influential venture firms: Thrive Capital, First Round Capital, Index Ventures, Inspired Capital, Able Partners, & more - firms that collectively manage more than $60 billion in assets & have backed some of the defining companies of the last decade. As always, expect live demos, sharp conversations, & a room full of founders, investors, engineers, & operators from across New York's tech ecosystem. Space is limited.

gary_2026_06_25_nyc_ai_coworking.title: NYC AI Coworking
gary_2026_06_25_nyc_ai_coworking.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_25_nyc_ai_coworking.summary: Gary's Guide: Spend the day at Station3 with other AI founders, engineers, designers, & operators for a focused co-working session built around getting work done & helping each other move faster. We will work from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with an afternoon demo / feedback block from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM. Demos stay short & practical: show what you are building, where you are stuck, & what kind of feedback, users, intros, or collaborators would help. This is for people actively building with AI: agents, developer tools, consumer products, media workflows, data products, infrastructure, creative tools, research workflows, & useful experiments. Polished demos are not required, but useful context is. Station3 is hosting us at 26 Broadway in FiDi. We will use Cedar, their large conference room, for demos & focused feedback, with the broader Station3 workspace supporting the co-working flow. What to bring: Laptop Charger Any demo, deck, repo, prototype, or product link you want feedback on A clear ask: users, feedback, intros, collaborators, GTM help, technical help, or hiring If you want to demo, mention what you are building in your RSVP or message Chris directly. We will prioritize builders who can show a real product, prototype, workflow, customer problem, or sharp question for the room. Join the NYC AI Co-working Telegram group for updates & builder asks: https://t.me/+KN-AraFGDkoyZDZh

gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.title: Product Summit
gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.summary: Gary's Guide: A one-day summit for product leaders & professionals at the edge of innovation. Themes being explored: Leading Product Strategy Through Disruption Frameworks for navigating market uncertainty, maintaining cross-functional alignment, & building resilient product roadmaps in rapidly evolving industries. Building Trust in AI-Driven Products Best practices for integrating intelligent systems & autonomous features while prioritizing user confidence, data privacy, & transparent user experiences. Unlocking Product-Led Growth & Retention How to build self-sustaining ecosystems by combining continuous user discovery, community engagement, & operational discipline to drive enduring product growth. "Shifting Left" in the Product Lifecycle Practical frameworks for accelerating time-to-value & reducing risk by bringing continuous discovery, technical feasibility, & quality assurance to the earliest stages of development. The Product Manager of the Future How autonomous agents, large language models, & advanced tooling are reshaping product workflows, strategic decision-making, & the PM's role in human-computer interaction.

gary_2026_06_25_after_hours_club_female_.title: After Hours Club - Female Founders Meditation & Art
gary_2026_06_25_after_hours_club_female_.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_25_after_hours_club_female_.summary: Gary's Guide: An After Hours Club night for female founders. We'll open with a short guided meditation to shake off the workday, then roll into a relaxed art session. No talent required, all supplies provided. Come meet other women building companies & make something with your hands that has nothing to do with your startup. Come as you are & leave feeling lighter, inspired, & more connected to yourself & like-minded community. Session will be held at the yoga studio in the gym. For Founders House & 22 Vanderbilt Members Only. Registration required. After Hours Club is a mental health-focused art & wellness community based in New York City. We create intentional spaces where conversations around mental health are normalized through guided workshops, creative expression, & genuine community connection. Women Who Lead is our ongoing series created to support female founders, entrepreneurs & leaders by giving them space to pause, reflect & recharge. Each gathering is thoughtfully curated to prioritize founders' mental health.

gary_2026_06_26_gen_juice_drinks_night_w.title: Gen&Juice - Drinks Night w/ AI Artists, Filmmakers, Tool Companies
gary_2026_06_26_gen_juice_drinks_night_w.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_26_gen_juice_drinks_night_w.summary: Gary's Guide: Are you an AI Filmmaker? Do you work for a Gen AI Company? Join us for drinks! Join us for a relaxed night of drinks, connections, & conversation with fellow AI filmmakers & startup founders who share a passion for pushing the boundaries of creative technology. This isn't one of our typical Gen Jams-think of it more like a casual get-together designed for meeting new collaborators, swapping insights on the latest tools, & simply having fun among like-minded peers. Whether you've just wrapped an AI-driven production, launched a startup, or are brainstorming your next big venture, you'll find plenty of new faces to connect with-and maybe even spark your next collaboration. So raise a glass & toast to the future of AI-powered cinema alongside the community that's shaping it!

gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.title: Founder-Led Growth - The Content System That Brings Customers
gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.summary: Gary's Guide: Agendan1:302:00 PM Arrival & Check-InnDoors open at 1:30 PM. Guests can arrive, settle in, & meet others before the first seminar begins. Early guests may also wait at the venue for the second session.n2:002:30 PM Seminar Session 1nA 30-minute seminar covering the main framework, examples, & takeaways.n2:302:45 PM Q&A for Session 1nAudience questions & discussion.n2:453:00 PM Intermission / Open ConversationnA short transition window before the second session begins.n3:003:30 PM Seminar Session 2nA repeat of the same 30-minute seminar for guests who prefer the later session.n3:303:45 PM Q&A for Session 2nAudience questions & discussion.n3:454:00 PM Open ConversationnGuests are welcome to stay after the seminars for casual conversation, follow-up questions, & deeper discussion.nNote: Seminar Session 2 is a repeat of Seminar Session 1. Guests only need to attend one session.nMost founders are told to post more content. That advice is expensive, vague, & usually wrong. If you're trying to grow a real business, content should not be a popularity contest, it should be a customer acquisition systemnThis seminar is a practical, direct look at how founders can use content to attract buyers, start conversations, & turn attention into revenue. Jun will walk through the core framework behind content that actually works, the kind that earns trust, creates demand, & brings in customers without wasting weeks guessing what to say nextnnThis session was widely well-received during New York Tech Week, & the response made one thing clear, founders are hungry for a better way to think about content. The ideas shared there helped cut through a lot of noise, & this time the conversation goes even deeper. Expect sharper examples, more usable tactics, & a clearer picture of how to build a content engine around growth, not vanitynnIf you've been treating content as a side task, a branding exercise, or a chore to keep up with, this event will challenge that thinking. The goal is simple, show you how to turn founder-led content into a repeatable system that supports sales & opens doorsnCommunity PartnernnSupermoonis a global network of 42,000 founders, builders, & investors, with over 200 in-person events hosted worldwide.nSupermoon operates Growth Engine, a NYC-based startup program supporting early-stage founders across deep tech, AI, fintech, & digital assets.

gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.title: Understanding Work, Agency & Stewardship In Age Of Generative & Agentic AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.summary: Gary's Guide: All Tech Is Human (ATIH), in partnership with the Notre Dame-IBM Tech Ethics Lab, will host a two-part workshop series focused on perceptions, adoption, & experience with Agentic AI systems across the workforce. Led by Jen Weedon, ATIH Braintrust member, this series aims to surface both strategic & lived insights from across organizational levels, generating a more holistic set of data points to inform responsible research & practice in the enterprise space. On Tuesday, July 14, the conversation moves toward organizational leadership & governance. We will bring together 20-30 senior executives who are currently standing at the crossroads of automation. Whether you are navigating the complexities of adoption, facing regulatory bottlenecks, dealing with internal reorgs or downsizing, and/or weighing the decision to automate core processes, this executive roundtable offers a high-level forum to discuss the ethics of efficiency & the push towards AI-first or AI-native transformations. This workshop aims to surface the process, knowledge, tensions, & skill gaps. Together, we will explore how leadership can drive innovation while maintaining the trust & integrity essential to the modern enterprise. Express your interest below. This workshop will be four hours long. The workshop series will explore how different types of organizations are approaching Agentic AI - from those whose systems are already deployed, to those preparing for rollout, to those still in exploratory phases. By comparing lived experiences with Agentic AI to expectations across roles & organizational contexts, the workshops will surface concerns, decision-making realities, & unintended consequences. The goal is to understand better the dynamics shaping acute & sustained adoption, governance, & the cascading effects on the workforce. Day 2: Agentic AI from the Leadership Perspective (Organizational & Ecosystem Leaders) Agenda forthcoming

gary_2026_07_15_quantum_frontiers_respon.title: Quantum Frontiers & Responsible AI - Global Dialogue On Emerging Tech
gary_2026_07_15_quantum_frontiers_respon.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_15_quantum_frontiers_respon.summary: Gary's Guide: All Tech Is Human & the Consulate General of Finland in New York are putting together a highly curated gathering for 75 individuals across civil society, government, industry, academia, & philanthropy. There will be two panel conversations & networking at the Finnish Consulate in New York, followed by a reception at the Finnish Residence near Central Park. Please express your interest below. All Tech Is Human takes a whole-of-ecosystem approach to tackling thorny tech & society issues & aligning our tech future with the public interest. We are building the crucial social infrastructure for the Responsible Tech ecosystem. Learn more at AllTechIsHuman.org & see all of our projects & initiatives here.

jc_culture_10003556_2026_06_24_hamilton_.title: Hamilton Park Farmers Market 2026
jc_culture_10003556_2026_06_24_hamilton_.label: Festival / Market
jc_culture_10003556_2026_06_24_hamilton_.place: Hamilton Park, 9 McWilliams Pl., Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_10003556_2026_06_24_hamilton_.summary: Join us at the Hamilton Park Farmers Market, your midweek destination for fresh, local, and thoughtfully crafted food and goods. Each week, we’re proud to feature farm-fresh produce from Stony Hill Farm alongside a curated selection of specialty vendors. Browse a variety of offerings including our long time vendor Pickles and Olives, fresh seafood from Scale Fish Market, and prepared foods perfect for dinner. You can also find baked goods, candles, personal care products, and more. You can even take advantage of convenient services like on-site knife sharpening while you shop. On the first and second Wednesdays of each month, enjoy live entertainment that adds to the vibrant, community atmosphere of the market. Whether you’re shopping for the week, grabbing a bite, or simply enjoying the park, the Hamilton Park Farmers Market is a welcoming space to connect with local vendors and neighbors.

jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.title: Playwriting Workshop for the History and Preservation Community
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.label: Art / Culture
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.place: Museum of Jersey City History, 298 Academy St., Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.summary: This workshop will encourage intersectionality and investigation as a way to write new plays based on aspects of history and preservation. Have you ever wanted to write a play? Have you found a niche piece of history that excites you? Do you want a space where you can write and talk about your favorite facts? Join M. D. Schaffer, playwright of “Drapetomania: A Negro Carol,” in a workshop that looks to give attendees the skills and structures to make art inspired by their historical interests. This is a collaboration between the Garden State New Play Festival, Jersey City Landmarks Conservancy and Museum of Jersey City History.

jc_culture_39999_2026_06_27_save_the_pow.summary: Our summer season of Save the Powerhouse Walking Tours starts on Saturday, June 27, 2026, 12-2pm. Explore the Hudson & Manhattan Railroad Powerhouse neighborhood with the Jersey City Landmarks Conservancy and experience a 200-year-old industrial landscape reinvented for 21st-century living and working. Co-sponsored by the Powerhouse Arts District Neighborhood Association (PADNA).

jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.title: Tirai – A Solo Bharatanatyam Showcase | July 4th, Jersey City
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.label: Art / Culture
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.place: ART HOUSE, 345 MARIN BLVD, JERSEY CITY
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.summary: Bharatanatyam in its most intimate form – one dancer, live Carnatic music, and the eternal question of what lies beyond the veil. Tirai is a solo Bharatanatyam showcase performed by Sneha, a dancer and teacher with nearly 30 years of practice, accompanied by a live Carnatic ensemble. The performance traces the soul’s journey through invocation, surrender, and the dissolution of illusion – weaving together classical movement, rhythm, and storytelling in an intimate black box setting. Bharatanatyam is a classical Indian dance tradition rooted in centuries of art, music, and devotion, and Tirai brings this living tradition to Jersey City in a deeply personal offering. This show is for anyone who loves the arts, is curious about Indian classical dance, or simply wants to start their Independence Day with something meaningful and beautiful. No prior knowledge of Bharatanatyam is needed – just an open heart.

jc_culture_40201_2026_07_04_july_4th_fir.title: July 4th Fireworks
jc_culture_40201_2026_07_04_july_4th_fir.label: Festival / Market
jc_culture_40201_2026_07_04_july_4th_fir.place: Exchange Place, MONTGOMERY ST BETWEEN HUDSON ST. AND GREEN ST.
jc_culture_40201_2026_07_04_july_4th_fir.summary: The full day of family-friendly festivities will once again draw tens of thousands of attendees, with over 60 vendors, 3 DJ stages, 2 beer gardens and a Kids’ Zone with activations and bounce castles. The Independence Day celebrations are capped off with breathtaking fireworks over the Hudson River with a picturesque backdrop of the NYC skyline. The celebration kicks off at 12:00 p.m. at Exchange Place. The night will conclude with a spectacular fireworks show, beginning at 9:30 p.m., lighting up the Hudson River synchronized to a patriotic soundtrack and visible across the waterfront. jc_culture_39112_2026_07_10_the_world_on.title: The World on Central 2026
jc_culture_39112_2026_07_10_the_world_on.label: Parks / Outdoor
jc_culture_39112_2026_07_10_the_world_on.place: Central Avenue, 370-437 Central Ave,Jersey, Jersey City
jc_culture_39112_2026_07_10_the_world_on.summary: “The World on Central” will activate one of Jersey City’s most diverse commercial corridors. In partnership with the Central Avenue Special Improvement District (CASID), this event is designed to drive foot traffic, promote local businesses, and position the Jersey City Heights as a regional destination for cultural celebration and economic activity. Event Policies No re-entry prohibited. Supervision required for children under 12. Event may be photographed or recorded. Allowed/Prohibited items for Event Prohibited Items Outside Food or Beverages Weapons/No CCPS Illegal Substances Drones Fireworks or Explosives Pets (except service animals) Lawn Chairs SECURITY SCREENING REQUIRED All guests are subject to magnetometer screening and bag inspection. Note that any items you bring to The World on Central could be subject to search. Please remember to keep bags, packages, strollers, etc. in sight. Do not leave any belongings unattended at any time.

jc_culture_40501_2026_07_11_earlybirds_c.summary: Join us for an unforgettable night at the **Earlybirds Club – Jersey City**! Set against the vibrant backdrop of White Eagle Hall, this live performance promises to be a celebration of music and community. The venue, known for its warm atmosphere and rich history, will come alive as the Earlybirds Club takes the stage, inviting you to immerse yourself in their captivating sound and infectious energy. Whether you’re a longtime fan or new to their music, this event is the perfect opportunity to connect with fellow music lovers in Jersey City. Don’t miss out on the chance to experience an evening filled with great vibes and unforgettable moments. Grab your friends and secure your spot at the **Earlybirds Club – Jersey City White Eagle Hall**—you won’t want to miss this!

Browse a variety of offerings including our long time vendor Pickles and Olives, fresh seafood from Scale Fish Market, and prepared foods perfect for dinner. You can also find baked goods, candles, personal care products, and more. You can even take advantage of convenient services like on-site knife sharpening while you shop. On the first and second Wednesdays of each month, enjoy live entertainment that adds to the vibrant, community atmosphere of the market. Whether you’re shopping for the week, grabbing a bite, or simply enjoying the park, the Hamilton Park Farmers Market is a welcoming space to connect with local vendors and neighbors.

jc_culture_40548_2026_07_15_how_many_com.title: How Many Comics Does It Take To Fix Everything?
jc_culture_40548_2026_07_15_how_many_com.label: Art / Culture
jc_culture_40548_2026_07_15_how_many_com.place: WHITE EAGLE HALL, 337 NEWARK AVE., JERSEY CITY
jc_culture_40548_2026_07_15_how_many_com.summary: Everything needs to be fixed! It’s like crazy out there. We’ve gathered as many comedians as possible to put the screws into all the nuts in the world. I mean, how many comedians could it take to fix… everything?! Hosted by Nick Fierro, Joshua Lay and Alex Grubard at White Eagle Hall.

jc_culture_10004634_2026_07_18_carringto.title: Carrington Village 5th Annual Carribean Festival
jc_culture_10004634_2026_07_18_carringto.label: Festival / Market
jc_culture_10004634_2026_07_18_carringto.place: Carrington Village, 459 Martin Luther King Dr., Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_10004634_2026_07_18_carringto.summary: The Carrington Village Caribbean Festival returns on July 18, 2026. 5 years of culture, and VIBES!!! ​During this event we will enchant Jersey City with Caribbean flavors, highlighting West Indian culture, food, artists, and entertainment with various activities for the entire family. The event is FREE for all. Kids Zone, Music, Food, and Culture Performaces by: Boom Boom Room Rhythm Section Ekkquinox Steel Band CarNYval Dancer Mas by Urban Dance and Sugar Candy Mas Special Traditional performance by Le Troupe Zetwal (Haiti) Musical Performances by: Mesha Steele (from Jamaica), Fiyah B (from Barbados) and Erphaan Alves (From Trinidad and Tobago)

jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.title: Around the World Watch Party 2026
jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.label: Parks / Outdoor
jc_culture_39108_2026_07_19_around_the_w.summary: The “Around the World Watch Party” is a festival centered on a live broadcast of the World Cup Final. The event will transform Claremont Avenue (between West Side Avenue and Mallory Avenue) into an immersive “world village,” featuring curated cultural zones representing different regions. This program is designed to bring together residents, local businesses, and cultural organizations in a free and accessible public space that promotes community engagement and cultural exchange. Event Policies No re-entry prohibited. Supervision required for children under 12. Event may be photographed or recorded. Allowed/Prohibited items for Event Prohibited Items Outside Food or Beverages Weapons/No CCPS Illegal Substances Drones Fireworks or Explosives Pets (except service animals) Lawn Chairs SECURITY SCREENING REQUIRED All guests are subject to magnetometer screening and bag inspection. Note that any items you bring to the Around the World Watch Party could be subject to search. Please remember to keep bags, packages, strollers, etc. in sight. Do not leave any belongings unattended at any time.
